product.name: Hydrate
product.tagline: Bottle-first water tracking for daily use.

hero.headline: Hydration tracking built around one bottle at a time.
hero.subhead: Hydrate is a phone-first water tracker that turns daily logging into a visible ritual with quick intake taps, a filling bottle, compact stats, and a local-first record.

summary: Hydrate focuses on the small actions that make a water tracker usable every day: one bottle, quick presets, adjustable goals, local persistence, streaks, and color rewards that do not depend on an account system.
audience: People who want a phone-first hydration log instead of a dense health dashboard
Users who prefer local-first tracking with no visible backend dependency in this repo
Anyone who responds better to visual progress and short reward loops than long forms
coreProblems: Many hydration tools bury the main signal behind too much surrounding chrome
Manual intake logging only works if it can happen in a few taps
Progress is easier to read when the tracker maps directly to a single visible bottle

features.title: Bottle-first feedback
features.summary: The core view uses one visible bottle, a progress ring, and a completed bottle counter rather than stacking multiple competing panels.

features.title: Sticky intake actions
features.summary: The current UI exposes preset intake amounts plus a centered custom amount entry from a sticky bottom action bar.

features.title: Compact trend view
features.summary: Recent totals, best day, and streak logic stay close to the main use case instead of becoming a full analytics dashboard.

features.title: Color unlock loop
features.summary: Logged water earns coins that can unlock bottle outline colors and alternate water tones.

features.title: Goal and unit control
features.summary: Daily goal and displayed units can change while storage remains normalized in mL.

workflows.title: Log intake in passing
workflows.summary: Use the preset amounts or custom entry to register water without leaving the main bottle view.

workflows.title: Tune the tracker to your routine
workflows.summary: Adjust the goal and display units so the bottle and totals match the way you already measure water.

workflows.title: Review recent consistency
workflows.summary: Check seven-day totals, best day, and streak length to see whether the habit is holding.

workflows.title: Change the feel of the bottle
workflows.summary: Preview and unlock bottle or water colors through the built-in shop flow.

proofPoints.label: Installable surface
proofPoints.note: The README explicitly describes the app as installable and offline-capable via Vite PWA.

proofPoints.label: Native shell
proofPoints.note: The repo includes a Capacitor iOS wrapper and Xcode project for developer builds.

proofPoints.label: Default goal
proofPoints.note: The default settings file sets the initial goal to 2000 mL.

proofPoints.label: Quick logging
proofPoints.note: The intake bar currently exposes 100, 250, 500, and 1000 mL plus a custom modal.

proofPoints.label: History windows
proofPoints.note: The stats view shows the last seven days, best day across fourteen days, and streaks across thirty days.

faq.question: Is this already an App Store release?
faq.answer: No App Store release is evidenced in the repo. The public-safe claim is that an iOS developer build project exists via Capacitor.

faq.question: Where is the data stored?
faq.answer: The visible state model stores the app data locally in the browser or device context through one persisted state object.

faq.question: Which units are supported?
faq.answer: The interface supports mL and US fl oz, with storage normalized in mL.

faq.question: Are screenshots available?
faq.answer: No screenshots were found in the repo, so this export uses custom diagrams based on the icon and observable feature set.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated Hydrate poster showing bottle tracking, a progress ring, stats bars, and unlockable colors.
screenshots.caption: Catalog visual derived from the repo icon and current feature surface.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated workflow board showing intake presets, bottle progression, stats bars, and settings.
screenshots.caption: Reference diagram used in place of screenshots that are not present in the repo.

catalog.title: Hydrate
catalog.blurb: A bottle-first hydration tracker with local history, streaks, and collectible color palettes.

repoArchetypeReasoning: The README explicitly describes the project as a phone-first water tracking app.
The current UI code centers on bottle fill feedback, daily logging, stats, units, streaks, and a built-in cosmetic shop.
Capacitor config and an iOS Xcode project indicate a mobile-facing direction beyond the browser.
assumptions: The safest public framing is a mobile app product overview rather than an app-store page because no store release metadata is present.
Support and privacy URLs are left blank because no public support or privacy documents were found in the repo.
Custom SVG diagrams are used because no screenshots were available.
missingButUsefulAssets: Real device screenshots from the bottle, stats, shop, and settings views
A dedicated social share image if the central site wants raster OG assets
Public support and privacy documents for final site hookup
